I've read all the docs thoroughly. Here's my analysis:
Project: ProctorWorks Offline Operations Platform
Stack: NestJS + TypeORM + PostgreSQL, Docker, pure backend (no UI)
13 slices to build, from foundation to final polish. The repo/ folder is empty
— building from scratch.
Key Domains
1. Auth/RBAC — 4 roles, JWT, bcrypt, account lockout
2. Rooms/Zones/Seats — hierarchy, versioned seat maps (immutable on publish)
3. Reservations — 15-min hold expiry, background cleanup job
4. Commerce — SPU/SKU, categories, brands, tiered pricing, inventory lots,
orders with state machine
5. Marketing — promotions + coupons, deterministic conflict resolution
(priority → best value → lower UUID)
6. Assessments — questions, papers, auto-grading, redo with history chain
7. Quality/Observability — data quality scores, freshness monitoring,
notifications
8. Security — AES-256-GCM encryption, audit masking, append-only audit log,
trace IDs
Additional Ambiguities to Add to questions.md
I noticed a few gaps the existing Q&A doesn't cover:
1. Stores CRUD — The prompt mentions stores for multi-tenant isolation, but no
explicit CRUD endpoints in api-spec. Should Platform Admin manage stores?
(I'll assume yes — basic CRUD needed)
2. Seat status changes — Can seats transition between any statuses freely, or
are there restricted transitions? (I'll assume free transitions via PATCH)
3. Order fulfill endpoint — The API self-test checklist lists POST
/orders/:id/fulfill but the api-spec doesn't define it explicitly. (I'll add
it)
4. Coupon distribute vs claim — The prompt mentions "distribute" but there's
no distribute endpoint. (I'll assume distribute = admin assigns coupon to
user, claim = user claims it)
5. SKU price tiers endpoint — The data model has sku_price_tiers but no
explicit CRUD endpoint. (I'll handle it as nested within SKU creation/update)
Let me add these to questions.md and then start building Slice 1.

149  **Assumption:** Design target enforced through proper indexing
, query optimization, and connection pooling. Not tested with
load tests in the submission, but documented in the README wit
h the indexing strategy.
150
151  **Solution:** Add database indexes on all foreign keys and fre
quently queried columns. Use TypeORM query builder for complex
queries to avoid N+1. Document index strategy in `docs/design
.md`.
152 +
153 +---
154 +
155 +## 16. Stores CRUD — Does Platform Admin Manage Stores?
156 +
157 +**Question:** The data model has a `stores` table and store_ad
+min is scoped to a store, but the API spec doesn't define expl
+icit store CRUD endpoints. Should Platform Admin be able to cr
+eate/list/update/delete stores?
158 +
159 +**Assumption:** Yes — Platform Admin needs basic CRUD for stor
+es so that store_admin users can be assigned to them. Endpoint
+s: `GET /stores`, `POST /stores`, `PATCH /stores/:id`, `DELETE
+ /stores/:id` (Platform Admin only).
160 +
161 +**Solution:** Add a `stores` module with basic CRUD, protected
+ by Platform Admin role.
162 +
163 +---
164 +
165 +## 17. Seat Status Transitions — Free or Restricted?
166 +
167 +**Question:** Can seats transition between any statuses (avail
+able, disabled, maintenance) freely, or are there restricted s
+tate transitions?
168 +
169 +**Assumption:** Free transitions — a `PATCH /seats/:id` with `
+{status: "maintenance"}` is allowed from any current status. T
+he only enforcement is that a seat in maintenance cannot have
+active holds.
170 +
171 +**Solution:** PATCH endpoint allows any valid status value. If
+ transitioning to maintenance, any active holds on the seat sh
+ould be expired.
172 +
173 +---
174 +
175 +## 18. Order Fulfill Endpoint — Missing from API Spec
176 +
177 +**Question:** The self-test checklist (Section 5) lists `POST
+/orders/:id/fulfill` but it's not in the API spec YAML. Should
+ it exist?
178 +
179 +**Assumption:** Yes — the state machine is pending → confirmed
+ → fulfilled → cancelled, so a fulfill endpoint is required. `
+POST /orders/:id/fulfill` transitions from confirmed to fulfil
+led.
180 +
181 +**Solution:** Add the endpoint to the orders controller alongs
+ide confirm and cancel.
182 +
183 +---
184 +
185 +## 19. Coupon Distribute — What Does It Mean?
186 +
187 +**Question:** The prompt mentions "distribute" as a coupon ope
+ration alongside claim, redeem, and expire. The API spec has a
+ claim endpoint but not distribute. What is distribute?
188 +
189 +**Assumption:** Distribute = admin assigns a coupon to specifi
+c users (batch operation). `POST /coupons/:id/distribute` with
+ `{userIds: [...]}` creates coupon_claims records for each use
+r without them needing to claim it themselves.
190 +
191 +**Solution:** Add distribute endpoint that creates claims for
+specified users.
192 +
193 +---
194 +
195 +## 20. SKU Price Tiers — Endpoint or Nested?
196 +
197 +**Question:** The data model has `sku_price_tiers` as a separa
+te table, but there's no explicit endpoint for managing price
+tiers. Should they have their own CRUD or be managed inline wi
+th SKU create/update?
198 +
199 +**Assumption:** Managed inline — when creating or updating a S
+KU, include a `priceTiers` array in the request body. No stand
+alone price tier endpoints.
200 +
201 +**Solution:** SKU create/update DTOs accept optional `priceTie
+rs: [{tierName, priceCents}]`. Service handles upsert/delete o
+f tiers within the SKU transaction.

could theoretically both succeed. A real fix would be a SELECT ... FOR UPDATE
or unique partial index. However, this isn't flagged in the acceptance audit
and the prompt says 50 req/s on a single-machine deployment — race window is
small.
